name: Guadalix Canyon and Aqueducts Trail
name_original: Rodeando el Cañón del Río Guadalix — Sierra de Guadarrama
description: This moderate 21.3-mile mountain biking route explores the scenic Guadalix River canyon, featuring waterfalls, aqueducts, and reservoir view
region_intro: Mountain biking around San Agustín Del Guadalix offers diverse natural landscapes, including the protected Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares. The region is characterized by the Guadalix River canyon, historic aqueducts, and extensive holm oak and cork oak forests. Riders can explore varied terrain, from riverbanks to areas with views of the distant Sierra de Guadarrama. This area provides a network of routes suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around San Agustín Del Guadalix The most popular mountain bike trail is Guadalix Canyon and Aqueducts Trail, a 21.3 miles (34.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route combines natural beauty with historical engineering, passing by impressive aqueducts. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ford of the Jarama River – Bridge over the Jarama River loop from Av. Principal - Urb. Valdelagua, a moderate 28.4 miles (45.7 km) path. This trail showcases varied landscapes along the Jarama River, transitioning from open fields to dense vegetation. Local mountain bikers also love the Retuerta Aqueduct – Hervidero Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix, a 17.1 miles (27.5 km) trail leading through the Guadalix River canyon and past the Cascada del Hervidero, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Mountain biking around San Agustín Del Guadalix is defined by the Guadalix River canyon, historic aqueducts, and holm oak forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore San Agustín Del Guadalix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: San Agustín Del Guadalix offers a wide network of mountain bike trails. You can find over 50 routes, catering to various skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix cater to all levels. There are 10 easy routes, 45 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can visit while mountain biking?
A: Yes, the region is home to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; on the Guadalix River, a popular spot accessible via several routes. Another notable waterfall is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6317937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Coso Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I see historical aqueducts along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1344851401/guadalix-canyon-and-aqueducts-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalix Canyon and Aqueducts Trail&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate the impressive 19th-century hydraulic infrastructures of the Canal de Isabel II, including the La Retuerta, Valcaliente, and La Sima aqueducts, adding a unique historical element to your ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for families, particularly the easier trails that follow riverbanks or less technical terrain. The Vía Verde del río Guadalix, for instance, is ideal for both cycling and walking, offering a gentle experience.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes will I encounter on the trails?
A: You'll experience diverse landscapes, including the scenic Guadalix River canyon, extensive holm oak and cork oak forests like the Bosque de la Dehesa Vieja, and open vistas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4905960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also part of or borders the protected Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in San Agustín Del Guadalix are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10428873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retuerta Aqueduct – Hervidero Waterfall loop from San Agustín del Guadalix&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic circular ride through the canyon.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the combination of natural beauty with historical elements like the aqueducts, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, you can find several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/649628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Guadalix Canyon from Matahonda Rocks&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning perspectives of the river canyon.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in San Agustín Del Guadalix?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler, wetter days.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Jarama River?
A: Yes, you can explore routes along the Jarama River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10442667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ford of the Jarama River – Bridge over the Jarama River loop from Av. Principal - Urb. Valdelagua&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate path that showcases varied landscapes, transitioning from open agricultural fields to lush, dense vegetation along the river.

Q: Are there options for more adventurous or technical mountain biking?
A: While many routes are moderate, the region also offers trails for more adventurous riders. Specific enduro mountain biking trails like 'Atalaya,' 'Majarrubia,' and 'Chorro de la Negra' are known for their technical descents and challenges, providing an exciting experience for advanced cyclists.
tips: Spectacular path that from the meadow of Moncalvillo brings us to San Agustín through a beautiful forest
Very good area to practice MTB, fast fun descents and with a point of technique.
A recent aqueduct, very close to the beautiful "La Cancheras" singletrail. Good place to take a break.
It consists of seven arches, also semicircular, which rise above a large lower arch.
Descent with two parts, the first part a wide track with loose stone and some jumps in the margins, up to a fence. The second part is a path with some unevenness and steps with stones.
A break on the route in a lagoon that changes according to the season of the year.

From this point, located on one of the CYII paths, you can enjoy the view of the Retuerta aqueduct and the entire Guadalix river canyon. There is a small path that allows you to get closer and go over the aqueduct, highly recommended although not suitable for people with vertigo.
A quiet place to relax. It is a reservoir which was completed in 1967.

The Church of San Miguel Arcángel de Pedrezuela was built in the 16th century, it is rectangular in shape.

It has two naves separated by semicircular arches. It is in the late Gothic style. It has two fonts of holy water from the late 15th century.

https://todosobremadrid.com/que-hacer/iglesia-de-san-miguel-arcangel-de-pedrezuela/
This beautiful mouse trail suddenly opens up to us with incredible views over the Guadalix canyon, and in the distance, the Zegri aqueduct.

The Guadalix River canyon, exploited by the Canal de Isabel II and with a dam in its highest part, is the perfect environment to explore, thanks to its terraced road in its eastern part. The Hervidero waterfalls are the ideal place to cool your feet and enjoy a meal accompanied by the freshness of nature. Its views do not disappoint, inviting us to enter its walls and captivate us with the greenery that permeates its slopes.

faqs.question: Where does the mountain biking trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in San Agustín de Guadalix. You'll find parking available in the town, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This moderate mountain biking route typically takes around 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the technical difficulty of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This is considered a moderate mountain bike ride, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. On the Single Track Scale (STS), it aligns with an S2 rating, meaning you should be comfortable with some technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a variety of surfaces, including fun roads and trails around the Pedrezuela Reservoir. The route also navigates through pasture landscapes and paths within the gorges carved by the Guadalix River, including a notable terraced road on the eastern side of the canyon.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several highlights, including the scenic Navalperal Aqueduct and Retuerta Aqueduct. You'll also pass by the Pedrezuela Reservoir and can enjoy views from the View of the La Retuerta Aqueduct and Mirador Atalaya el Molar Spectacular sunsets. A notable natural feature is the Hervidero waterfalls, an ideal spot to cool off.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Guadarrama region is generally dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and other trail users. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this mountain biking trail around the Canyon of the Guadalix River.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Guadarrama region is rich in wildlife. Along the route, you might spot deer, wild boars, and foxes. The area is also home to various birds of prey, including the imperial eagle and black vulture.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other paths in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You'll ride along parts of the Avenida de Madrid, Camino del Carril de las Mentiras, Camino de Guadalix, Canal de El Mesto, Camino Canal Bajo CYII, Canal Bajo, and Camino Canal del Vellón.

faqs.question: Are there any specific tips for riding this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and technical sections, ensure your bike is in good condition and you have appropriate gear. The route circles the Pedrezuela Reservoir, offering consistent scenic views. Don't miss the Hervidero waterfalls for a refreshing stop.
